questions.question: Explain the four main components of an imaging system with their functions.
keyPoints: Image Acquisition - captures light and converts to electrical signals using sensors like CCD/CMOS
Image Processing - enhances, filters, and manipulates image data using hardware/software
Image Storage - saves image data in various formats (JPEG, PNG, TIFF) on memory devices
Image Display - renders digital images on monitors, printers, or other output devices

questions.question: Differentiate between CCD and CMOS image sensors.
keyPoints: CCD: Higher image quality, lower noise, sequential readout, higher power consumption, more expensive
CMOS: Lower cost, lower power, random pixel access, but higher noise levels
CCD preferred in professional imaging; CMOS in consumer devices

questions.question: What is sampling and quantization in digital image acquisition? Explain their significance.
keyPoints: Sampling: Converting continuous spatial domain to discrete pixels - determines spatial resolution
Quantization: Converting continuous intensity values to discrete levels - determines color/bit depth
Together they convert analog image to digital form; affect image quality and file size

questions.question: Compare RGB and CMYK color models.
keyPoints: RGB: Additive model using Red, Green, Blue; used in monitors, cameras, displays
CMYK: Subtractive model using Cyan, Magenta, Yellow, Black; used in printing
RGB combines light to create colors (0+0+0=black, max+max+max=white)
CMYK combines inks to absorb light (white paper + all inks = black)

questions.question: Explain the concept of bit depth in digital images with examples.
keyPoints: Bit depth = number of bits per pixel determining color/shade variations
1-bit = 2 colors (black/white)
8-bit = 256 gray levels (grayscale)
24-bit = 16.7 million colors (true color)
Higher bit depth = more color detail, larger file size

questions.question: List and explain common image file formats and their characteristics.
keyPoints: JPEG: Lossy compression, small size, good for photographs
PNG: Lossless compression, transparency support, good for graphics
BMP: Uncompressed, large file size, good for editing
TIFF: High quality, lossless, used in printing
GIF: Limited colors (256), supports animation

questions.question: What is image resolution? Explain different types of resolution.
keyPoints: Spatial Resolution: Number of pixels (e.g., 1920×1080) - more pixels = more detail
Bit Depth/Color Resolution: Number of colors/shades (e.g., 24-bit)
Temporal Resolution: Frame rate in video (fps)
Spectral Resolution: Color bands in multispectral imaging

questions.question: Explain the process of digital image acquisition. Discuss the role of image sensors and the steps involved in converting a real-world scene to a digital image.
keyPoints: Light from scene enters through lens onto image sensor
Sensor (CCD/CMOS) converts photons to electrical charges
Sampling: Sensor array defines pixel positions (spatial discretization)
Quantization: ADC converts analog signals to digital values (amplitude discretization)
Color filter array (Bayer pattern) captures color information
Signal processing applies corrections and compression
Final digital image stored as matrix of pixel values
Key concepts: Spatial resolution, bit depth, color representation

questions.question: Describe the fundamentals of digital image representation. Include discussion on pixels, bit depth, color models, and the mathematical representation of images.
keyPoints: Digital image = 2D array of values f(x,y) where x=row, y=column
Pixel = smallest element with intensity/color values
Grayscale: Single value per pixel (0=black, 255=white for 8-bit)
Color: Multiple channels (R,G,B values for RGB model)
Bit depth determines range: n-bit = 2^n possible values
Image matrix: f(x,y) for grayscale; [Rr(x,y), Gg(x,y), Bb(x,y)] for color
Resolution = M×N pixels; Aspect ratio = width/height

questions.question: Explain the working principles of CCD and CMOS image sensors. Compare their advantages, disadvantages, and applications.
keyPoints: CCD: Photodiode collects charge, transferred pixel by pixel to output amplifier - sequential readout
CMOS: Each pixel has its own amplifier and ADC - random access
CCD Advantages: Higher QE, lower noise, better image quality
CCD Disadvantages: Higher power, expensive, slower readout
CMOS Advantages: Low power, cheap, fast readout, integrated electronics
CMOS Disadvantages: Higher noise, lower fill factor
Applications: CCD in scientific cameras, medical imaging; CMOS in mobile phones, consumer cameras

questions.question: Discuss the concept of color representation in digital imaging. Explain various color models and their applications.
keyPoints: Color models mathematically represent colors for digital systems
RGB: Additive, used in emissive displays - colors formed by combining R,G,B light
CMYK: Subtractive, used in printing - colors formed by absorbing light with inks
HSI: Hue (color type), Saturation (purity), Intensity (brightness) - intuitive for humans
YUV: Luminance (Y) and Chrominance (U,V) - used in video transmission
LAB: Perceptually uniform - used in color correction
Each model suited for specific applications based on how colors are created or displayed
